title: thymophylla tenuiloba var. texana
intro: Thymophylla tenuiloba var. texana, also known as the Texas pineland daisy, is a plant species native to Texas and parts of Mexico. It is a perennial herb that belongs to the Asteraceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain, prevent infection, and boost the immune system, providing health benefits such as wound healing and relief from fever. Thymophylla tenuiloba var. texana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
